title: Unauthenticated Basket IDOR on a High-Traffic E-Commerce Platform
summary: Identified an unauthenticated cross-user basket read vulnerability via GraphQL IDOR on cultbeauty.co.uk. Basket UUIDs assigned pre-authentication allowed any actor to read another user's basket contents with zero credentials. Confirmed valid by program; closed as duplicate of a 2024 report on a now out-of-scope target.

timeline.summary: Identified GraphQL endpoints, confirmed introspection enabled, retrieved full schema dump across 300+ types.

Systematically tested high-priority mutations and queries. deleteAddress, cancelOrder, and logoutSession found to be properly authenticated. replaceAddress produced internal server error on cross-user ID.
Confirmed unauthenticated cross-user basket read. customer: null in response proves zero authentication required. Basket IDs confirmed to be assigned pre-authentication via localStorage.

Tested basket checkout mutation for race condition and hijack vectors. Attempted to query richer fields (delivery address, vouchers, gift cards). Impact ceiling established at shopping intent data only.
Finding submitted to Bugcrowd as P3. Assessed as P4 and marked duplicate of 2024 report on a now out-of-scope target.
Engagement concluded. Vulnerability confirmed valid by duplicate classification. Codebase-wide exposure noted across malatan.co.uk and enjoyselfish.com.

confidentiality.summary: Shopping intent data (SKUs, quantities, prices) exposed across all user sessions including guests. No PII, payment data, or account credentials accessible.

integrity.summary: Cross-user basket merge observed when authenticated user submits another user's basket UUID. Item state mutations reflect across shared basket sessions.

availability.summary: Basket state interference between sessions could disrupt a user's active shopping or checkout flow.

privacy.summary: Shopping behaviour and product interest data exposed without consent. Affected population includes all site visitors, not only registered users.

regulatory.summary: Potential UK GDPR consideration given exposure of behavioural data (purchasing intent) without authentication. No special category data involved.

operational.summary: No material operational disruption identified. Basket interference is incidental rather than systematic.

lessons.summary: Check program disclosed reports and duplicate history before deep-diving on a finding. A 2024 report on a shared codebase made this submission a duplicate despite the finding being valid and independently discovered.

In multi-target programs with a shared codebase, test the same vector across all in-scope targets before submitting. A program-wide finding affecting multiple named domains carries higher severity and lower duplicate risk.

Establish the impact ceiling before submission rather than after. Escalation testing (voucher codes, delivery address, checkout race condition) added ~2 hours without producing additional submittable impact.

Pre-authentication object creation (guest baskets, guest sessions) significantly broadens IDOR impact and is frequently overlooked. Always test ID-based queries from a zero-auth baseline before adding session context.

IDORs on low-sensitivity data (shopping carts, wishlists) without PII or payment data will trend toward P4 regardless of the elegance of the exploit chain. Know the severity line before investing time in a finding.

seo.description: Case study: GraphQL basket IDOR on cultbeauty.co.uk allowing unauthenticated read of any user's shopping basket. Confirmed valid by Bugcrowd, closed as duplicate.
